education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science

description: Achieved zero critical incidents across weekly releases to 50M+ users (measured by Sentry error rates and App Store stability metrics) by owning end-to-end iOS release cycles and on-call workflows, using Instruments to profile and resolve performance bottlenecks before production

Generated $100K+ in revenue within 10 days (measured by A/B test conversion analytics) by building the Deal Center feature end-to-end in Swift, SwiftUI, and Combine across paywall and pricing experiment flows

Improved onboarding completion rate by 15% (measured by funnel analytics) by rebuilding onboarding flows with Lottie and ReactiveSwift, and implementing VoiceOver accessibility across 30+ screens (~90% coverage)

Reduced technical debt across 18K+ lines of legacy code (measured by SonarQube maintainability scores) by migrating Objective-C/UIKit surfaces to Swift/SwiftUI under MVVM, with SwiftLint standards enforced

Accelerated release documentation turnaround by 60% (measured by release cycle time) by building internal AI-assisted tooling that automated release notes generation while maintaining compliance standards
Reduced API-related crashes by 25% (measured by Sentry network error tracking) by refactoring REST API integrations with Alamofire, centralized error handling, and retry logic

experience.role: Lead Graduate Teaching Assistant - Theory of Computation
description: Led Theory of Computation as Lead TA across two semesters for 200+ graduate students, owning weekly discussion sections, exam-prep resources, and rubric calibration
Mentored junior TAs on grading consistency, office-hour facilitation, and student communication

Maintained 99.9% uptime during critical production incidents by debugging enterprise systems on-site for clients, delivering hot fixes in C++ and Python, and querying MySQL databases to trace root causes under live production conditions

Improved data pipeline reliability for 30% of enterprise customers (measured by reduced data loss incidents) by designing and iterating on the EventStreamer module with a secure, loss-resistant architecture suitable for regulated environments

Reduced average issue resolution time by 50% (measured by internal ticketing metrics) by building Python and shell automation for hotfix deployment, log parsing, and triage workflows, cutting manual steps in the support-to-engineering escalation loop

Drove organization-wide adoption of standardized deployment patterns (measured by team onboarding metrics) by containerizing HX platform microservices with Docker and Compose, resolving x86-to-ARM64 compatibility issues for the team's laptop migration

projects.title: NLQ - Natural Language Queries Over Your Database
projects.description: Ask any SQLite database questions in plain English, privately and locally. Runs on top of Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama, vLLM, or Apple Intelligence on-device, is read-only by default, and ships as a CLI, a Streamlit playground, and a Model Context Protocol server so Claude Desktop can query the database directly.
projects.problem: NL to SQL tools either silently hallucinate when they don't know an answer, demand you ship your schema to a third party, or lock you to one model vendor. None of those is acceptable when the database holds real data and you want on-device privacy.
projects.approach: Built a provider-agnostic NL to SQL pipeline behind a single LLMProvider protocol so Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama, vLLM, and Apple Intelligence are one config flag apart. Every generation is constrained to one of three states (ANSWER with SQL, CANNOT_ANSWER with reason, or CLARIFY with a follow-up question), validated with sqlglot, and auto-limited on unbounded reads. Read-only is the default; writes need an explicit flag. The same engine ships as a CLI, a Streamlit playground, and an MCP stdio server with a lazy-schema tool-calling mode that falls back to schema injection when the model can't use tools.
projects.impact: Zero silent hallucinations across a 35-question eval harness covering all three response states, with 164 unit tests running in roughly one second. Ships as a CLI, Streamlit playground, and MCP server, demo-ready against any SQLite database with a single command.
projects.nextSteps: PostgreSQL and MySQL adapters via the existing SchemaExtractor protocol slot, an optional NL result summarizer, and a multi-connection config manager so one binary can route across several databases.

projects.title: ProximityLink - BLE Dual-Mode iOS App
projects.description: Dual-mode Bluetooth Low Energy iOS app supporting real-time device scanning and beacon broadcasting via CoreBluetooth Central and Peripheral Manager APIs.
projects.problem: Apps that work with BLE typically pick one role (scanner or beacon) and one app state (foreground). Real proximity workflows need both roles and survive backgrounding, with UI that stays in sync as the radio reports changes every few hundred milliseconds.
projects.approach: Built dual-mode operation against CoreBluetooth's Central and Peripheral APIs with iOS background mode support. Distance is estimated with a log-distance path-loss model and bucketed into user-friendly proximity tiers from RSSI samples. A Combine pipeline streams hardware events directly into SwiftUI views so the list updates without manual refresh, and stale devices are pruned automatically when they stop advertising.
projects.impact: Stable scanning and broadcasting across foreground/background transitions, with a reactive UI that needs no refresh logic and graceful handling of Bluetooth permission and state errors.
projects.nextSteps: Add CoreLocation iBeacon ranging as a fallback in noisy RF environments, ship as a Swift Package for embedding in other apps, and add an in-app inspector that records and replays BLE traces for debugging on customer devices.

projects.title: ShapeTracer - Multimodal iOS Tracing App
projects.description: Native iOS app combining Core Haptics, AVFoundation, and custom CoreGraphics rendering in SwiftUI for motor-skill assessment and rehabilitation.
projects.problem: Tracing exercises used in motor-skill research typically lack tight, multimodal feedback. To be useful clinically, an iOS app needs to render strokes smoothly, react to stroke deviation in real time across visual / haptic / audio channels, and stay reliable enough to use in a study.
projects.approach: Built modular SwiftUI views over custom CoreGraphics rendering, layered Core Haptics and AVFoundation feedback on stroke events, and structured the codebase around SOLID boundaries so each feedback channel can be swapped or A/B tested. Validated the pipelines with XCTest, achieving 90%+ coverage on the rendering and feedback modules.
projects.impact: A reliable, testable iOS app that demonstrates advanced system integrations end-to-end and is ready to use as a research instrument.
projects.nextSteps: Ship to TestFlight for clinician evaluation, replace polling-based hit detection with Metal-rendered overlays for smoother tracing on older devices, and add a CoreML-based stroke-similarity score so results can be summarised per session.

projects.title: Stock Market Simulator for iOS
projects.description: Production-ready iOS trading simulator with real-time market data, MVVM architecture, and Firebase biometric auth.
projects.problem: Beginners learning to trade need a safe, realistic environment with current market data, secure accounts, and a UI that stays usable as positions and watchlists grow.
projects.approach: Built an MVVM iOS app pulling real-time data over REST APIs (Alamofire), integrated Firebase auth gated behind Face ID / Touch ID via LocalAuthentication, and structured portfolio state so account, watchlist, and trade flows are independently testable.
projects.impact: A secure simulator that supports a complete trade flow end-to-end with real market data and biometric-protected accounts.
projects.nextSteps: Migrate the UI layer to SwiftUI + MVVM-C, replace REST polling with WebSocket streams for live tickers, add APNs push notifications for per-symbol alerts, and persist portfolios in Core Data for offline access.

projects.title: Government ID Scanner for Northeastern Recreation
projects.description: Mobile-friendly ID scanner on Google Vision API auto-extracting data into a serverless backend on Cloud Run, deployed across multiple iPads at Northeastern Recreation. Reduced user registration time ~65% and cut operational costs ~70% by replacing manual entry with auto-scaling serverless infrastructure.

projects.title: Kubernetes Cluster Management with AWS Integration
projects.description: DevOps project demonstrating container orchestration and cloud infrastructure management. Full lifecycle deployment from local KIND environments to production-grade AWS EKS with Infrastructure as Code.

projects.title: Financial Data Streaming Backend
projects.description: Event-driven backend for real-time financial data polling, analytics, and streaming. Built on FastAPI, async SQLAlchemy, Kafka, Redis, and PostgreSQL for speed and scalability.

projects.title: DataForge - End-to-End Database Analytics Platform
projects.description: Data engineering project transforming raw CSV data into a relational database with analytical capabilities. Demonstrates the full data science pipeline from exploration to insights.

projects.title: Data Warehouse for Entertainment Distribution Merger
projects.description: Data warehousing project simulating a merger between two entertainment distribution companies. Advanced ETL processes, data modeling, and analytical reporting.

projects.title: QR-Based Image Upload for Moodle
projects.description: Security-focused Moodle plugin enabling secure image uploads through cryptographically-signed QR codes for online exams. Deployed for 1000+ students.

projects.title: This Portfolio Website
projects.description: This portfolio itself - yes, it made it to my projects section. Built with React, Tailwind, and TypeScript; auto-built and deployed to GitHub Pages via GitHub Actions on every push.

projects.title: Image Manipulation Software
projects.description: Comprehensive image processing application built around extensibility and maintainability. Demonstrates layered architecture, multiple design patterns, and TDD practices. Source code available on request.
